feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: CDS Custom entity - clicking on line item always shows the Header entity name
entry.content:
Hi,

I am developing a custom entity based RAP development which displays Job details in a header report. by clicking on the header row, it takes to the line item report. 

But the report always points to the Parent entity name when clicking on the line item report.

Request your support to identify the issue. Thank you in advance.

Best Regards,

Shankar Sanke.

entry.title: Define routes of app router to ui5 landing page
entry.content:
Hi everyone

I have a fullstack cap application with a managed approuter that will be used to route to multiple apps within my larger application. Currently I trying to get the approuter to route to a custom ui5 freestyle app (essentially custom tiles) that will be used to launch each of my required applications. However, the current approuter routes results in the following error: 

Cannot GET /baseui/webapp/index.html

Please note that my folder structure is as follows, with my freestyle ui5 app sitting under baseui>webapp>view>homeview.xml. My app router was generated automatically under the app folder. Can someone please highlight the mistakes I am making?

entry.title: Attachement is attached in the Fiori My Inbox, and I want to view the attachemnt in SAP or Fiori
entry.content:
Attachment is attached in the Fiori My Inbox, and I want to view the attachment in SAP or Fiori, But I could not view the Attached Document, Please provide me the solution.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

1. PR should created from Fiori
2. After Create a PR, Go to My Inbox for Approve.
3. In My Inbox select -> show Details in Right side        bottom -> and Select the Attachment.
4. Select the Attachment for the Upload and after Upload Approver will Approve the PR.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Defaulting filter value using user defaults for a custom sapui5 app
entry.content:
Hi All,
I have developed a custom EWM app, where I want to use user defaulted value for warehouse field in my app. I have setup user defaults on the launchpad and saved it. Also, I have used parameter in the tile and target mapping under /ui2/flpd_cust (Designer), but it is not working.

Target mapping config:

Tile Config:

Screen flow:

For my custom app, when i click on the main screen, it navigates to a KPI tile screen with multiple KPI tiles and on selecting on any of the tiles, it lands to an OVP app, that contains the warehouse filter (which I want to fetch from user defaults). 
Main tile -> KPI Tile screen (without filters) -> Overview page with filters.

Any help here, will be rewarded.

References:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/setting-user-defaults-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13341475

entry.title: How to find the Business Context of an application without documentation?
entry.content:
I am working on S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 (Private Cloud). I am trying to extend the app Manage Journal Entries (F0717) by adding custom fields to the "Line Items" section of the Journal Entry Item.

Fiori Apps Library Limitations: The "Extensibility" section for F0717 only mentions SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects, but does not list the underlying Backend Business Contexts for data model extension.

What is the formal technical procedure to identify which Business Context maps to a specific section of a Fiori App?

Is there a technical way to find the required Business Context by inspecting the OData Service?

entry.title: Pattern
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am trying to find an iOS pattern where the top navigation bar has a drop-down to navigate between pages. Could you please point me to the right resource for the same. 

Best, 

Janani

entry.title: Adaptation editor not showing navigation in Audit Universe
entry.content: I have created an adaptation project in BAS. However, upon opening the adaptation editor, I cannot see any of the data to make me navigate to the view in where I have to extend. Is this a limitation or am I missing actual roles? I already have all dev roles necessary and auditor roles.

entry.title: errors coming in the ui5 walkthrough yaml file for the northWind oData service
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Kindly check I am getting error on the hana.ondemandtrial server while running the walkthrough code.

Error: 

Error Message:

Failed to register backend for V2/Northwind/Northwind.svc/. Check configuration in yaml file.

Error: Destination not found. Please check your configuration or user role assignment.

Thanks

Kaushik Banerjee

SAP Fiori/SAP UI5/SAP Portal/SAP WDJ

entry.title: How to configure managed approuter to serve CAP endpoints?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a fullstack CAP cloud application along with a managed approuter. I am having some trouble getting the approuter to correctly point to my cap service (currently without a UI). Currently the approuter correctly points to my ui5 page (capbase) but my cap service (dev-capbase-srv) returns the error message 

Cannot GET / 

However, when I append the /odata/v4/catalog/Employees to my url I get a 401 unauthorized error. But I have tested this via postman by generating a bearer token and can then see the data displayed. But my question is why is my approuter not directing me to this service automatically without having to append the /odata/v4/catalog/Employee endpoint to my url: port4004-workspaces-ws-ak3uq.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/

Further information: I have a fiori list report generated in my app folder for this CAP employee service
my xs-app.json file is as follows:

{
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/v4/catalog/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/v4/catalog/$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "csrfProtection": false,
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ],
  "welcomeFile": "/interfacebase"
}

mta.yaml 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: capbase
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
- name: capbase-srv
  type: nodejs
  path: gen/srv
  requires:
  - name: capbase-auth
  provides:
  - name: srv-api
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    instances: 1
    memory: 256M
  build-parameters:
    builder: npm-ci
- name: capbase
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: app/router
  requires:
  - name: srv-api
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: true
      name: srv-api
      url: ~{srv-url}
  - name: capbase-auth
  - name: capbase-html5-runtime
  - name: capbase-destination-service
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: false
      name: ui5
      url: https://ui5.sap.com
  provides:
  - name: app-api
    properties:
      app-protocol: ${protocol}
      app-uri: ${default-uri}
  parameters:
    disk-quota: 256M
    keep-existing-routes: true
    memory: 256M
- name: capbase-app-deployer
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: gen
  requires:
  - name: capbase-auth
  - name: srv-api
  - name: capbase-html5-repo-host
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: app/
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - interfacebase.zip
      name: interfacebase
      target-path: app/
    - artifacts:
      - employeeui.zip
      name: employeeui
      target-path: app/
- name: interfacebase
  type: html5
  path: app/interfacebase
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
- name: employeeui
  type: html5
  path: app/employeeui
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: capbase-auth
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      oauth2-configuration:
        credential-types:
        - binding-secret
        - x509
        redirect-uris:
        - https://*~{app-api/app-uri}/**
      tenant-mode: dedicated
      xsappname: capbase-${org}-${space}
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-plan: application
  requires:
  - name: app-api
- name: capbase-html5-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-host
- name: capbase-html5-runtime
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-runtime
- name: capbase-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: capbase-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
  - builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm ci
    - npx cds build --production

entry.title: Returned Quantity Field Does Not Allow Decimal Values in Perform Maintenance Job App in FIORI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Please I have a question, In the Fiori app Perform Maintenance Job, the dialog Return Goods does not allow the entry of decimal values in the Returned Quantity field when returning components from a maintenance order.

The input field only accepts integer values, even for materials whose unit of measure supports decimal quantities.

 

 

Please someone have any solution?

Thank you,

Regards

entry.title: SAP BTP HTML5 App asking login again and again
entry.content:
Hello Community,
I have recently started working with RAP programming and successfully developed a Fiori application using CDS views, OData services, and annotation-based UI. The app was generated using the Fiori Generator in SAP Business Application Studio, and the preview runs without any issues.
I then deployed the application to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP. The deployment completed successfully, and I can see the app listed under the HTML5 Applications section. However, when I try to access the app, I am repeatedly prompted with a browser login dialog. Even after providing my valid BTP credentials, the prompt continues to reappear, preventing me from accessing the application.
I have followed the same steps as outlined in the learning resources, but I am unsure where the configuration might be missing or incorrect. Could you please guide me on what might be causing this repeated login prompt and how to resolve it?
Thank you in advance for your support.


Trust Config:

XS-APP.JSON:

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/sap/(.*)$",
      "target": "/sap/$1",
      "destination": "abap-cloud-abap-cc2459batrial-dev",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}
XS-SECURITY.JSON code:
{
  "xsappname": "travelapp",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "description": "Security profile of called application",
  "scopes": [],
  "role-templates": []
}
MTA.YAML:

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: travelapp
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
- name: travelapp-destination-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  requires:
  - name: travelapp-destination-service
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  - name: travelapp-repo-host
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: travelapp-repo-host-key
  - name: travelapp-uaa
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: travelapp-uaa-key
  parameters:
    content:
      instance:
        destinations:
        - Name: travelapp_html_repo_host
          ServiceInstanceName: travelapp-html5-service
          ServiceKeyName: travelapp-repo-host-key
          sap.cloud.service: travelapp
        - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
          Name: travelapp_uaa
          ServiceInstanceName: travelapp-xsuaa-service
          ServiceKeyName: travelapp-uaa-key
          sap.cloud.service: travelapp
        existing_destinations_policy: update
  build-parameters:
    no-source: true
- name: travelapp-app-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: travelapp-repo-host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - travelapp.zip
      name: travelapp
      target-path: resources/
- name: travelapp
  type: html5
  path: .
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: travelapp-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: travelapp-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
- name: travelapp-uaa
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-name: travelapp-xsuaa-service
    service-plan: application
- name: travelapp-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-name: travelapp-html5-service
    service-plan: app-host
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
SAP BTP ABAP environment SAP Fiori Makers

entry.title: Make Custom Field read-only that is added using Custom Fields and Logic Fiori app (F1481)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We added a Custom Field in Fiori app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843A) using Custom Field and Logic Fiori app (F1481) with Business Context Accounting: Coding Block (FINS_CODING_BLOCK) and enabled it for OData service's (MMIM_GR4PO_DL_SRV ) Item entity, GR4PO_DL_Item. Now, we want to set this field to Read-only but we are not able to find correct way to set it.

We noticed that when we created this custom field, another custom field was also created in the background of type CFD_CUSTOM_UI_FIELD_CONTROL for structure MMIM_GR4PO_DL_ITEM, which seems like a field for field properties control, but we are not sure how to use this or change the behaviour using this field. This additional field is not available/visible when we are writing logic in Custom Logic app (F6957), only the created custom field is shown.

Does anyone also faced this issue and can suggest how to proceed?

Best regards

Ankur

entry.title: Kontoauszug nachbearbeiten falsche Kombination
entry.content:
S/4HANA Privat Cloud beim Kontoauszug nachbearbeiten es kommt zur Fehlermeldung , Konto Hausbank und Bukr haben nicht die richtige Kombination.

Gibt es eine Übersicht (außer Sachkonten zu Kontensymbolen zuordnen) und wo kann ich das Konto zuordnen

VG

Floh1

entry.title: Custom UI5 tile for FLPD
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working on an SAP on-premise Fiori Launchpad and I am trying to add a custom UI5 tile (a KPI/ dynamic tile rendered by a custom UI5 component) to a catalog/ group so users can place it on the launchpad.

  • The tile was build in VS Code, and the UI5 app is deployed to ABAP as BSP
  • The tile content works in a standalone local preview

Is it actually possible on classic on-prem FLP to add a UI5 custom tile as a tile type in FLPD?

Any guidance, or pointers to the right direction would be very appreciated.

entry.title: Does anyone faced the SAP UI overlapping issue in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with UI overlapping in the Fiori launchpad build with SAP Build workzone. When user is trying to navigate between the applications within Fiori launchpad the screen is splitting into two and 2 apps are being displayed as overlapping. Need any experts help here to understand the issue and rectification.

entry.title: How to: Open Embedded Url added using Adapt UI in a new Window for SAP Fiori App ?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have embedded a new section pointing to an url, in the standard fiori app Manage Sales Order. However this url always opens in the same window within the section added. 

Our need was to open this url in a new browser window / tab. I have tried few parameters, however it doesn't seem to work and i can't find any sap standard way to achieve this. 

Please suggest if there is any way to achieve this requirement using Adapt UI.

Refer to below screen, where i try to add url in a section using Adapt UI, and it opens in same iFrame in same window.

@sapfiori @SAPSAP @Fiori 

Many Thanks,

RK

entry.title: Unable to Load SAP Shell plugin Locally(SAP UI5)
entry.content:
Hi All,
I'm encountering an issue with running SAP shell plugin changes locally. Every time I make updates, I have to deploy them in order to see the changes in the shell plugin.
I will be sharing my mainfest.json & component.js code for you reference.
I followed this sap tutorials to create my sap shell plugin - link
During the process, I found that I need to set the option Use Virtual Endpoints for Local Preview as No, but still I'm still getting the same error regardless of whether I Select Yes or No for this option.

Error (Both for yes and no option for Use Virtual Endpoints for Local Preview😞 - 

1. What could be the root cause of the issue? Is there something extra I need to add?
2. Why is it necessary to select No for "Use Virtual Endpoints for Local Preview"?
3. If I originally created the application with Yes for this setting, how can I change it to No?

mainfest.json code :- 

{
  "_version": "1.81.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "zplugin000",
    "type": "component",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:basic",
      "version": "1.20.2",
      "toolsId": "5f79ee28-1af6-428c-84a7-62830ac66232"
    },
    "crossNavigation": {
      "inbounds": {
        "Shell-plugin": {
          "semanticObject": "Shell",
          "action": "plugin",
          "hideLauncher": true,
          "signature": {
            "parameters": {},
            "additionalParameters": "allowed"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.144.1",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "zplugin000.i18n.i18n"
        }
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": [
        {
          "uri": "css/style.css"
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "zplugin000"
  },
  "sap.flp": {
    "type": "plugin"
  }
}

Component.js :- 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/UIComponent",
    "zplugin000/model/models",
    "sap/ushell/Container",
    "sap/m/Text",
], (UIComponent, models, Container, Text) => {
    "use strict";

    return UIComponent.extend("zplugin000.Component", {
        metadata: {
            manifest: "json",
            interfaces: [
                "sap.ui.core.IAsyncContentCreation"
            ]
        },

        init() {
            // call the base component's init function
            UIComponent.prototype.init.apply(this, arguments);

            // set the device model
            this.setModel(models.createDeviceModel(), "device");

            // enable routing
            this.getRouter().initialize();

            // enable footer
            this.setupFooter();
        },
        setupFooter: async function () {
            const FrameBoundExtension = await Container.getServiceAsync("FrameBoundExtension");
            let nCurrentYear = new Date().getFullYear();
            await FrameBoundExtension.createFooter({
                id: "flpFooter",
                contentLeft: [
                    new Text({
                        id: "copyrightStatement",
                        text: this.getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle().getText("copyrightStatement", [nCurrentYear])
                    })
                ]
            });
        },
    });
});

SAPUI5  #Shell Plugin SAP Fiori Makers

entry.title: Fiori My Inbox: WITH_URL Annotation Always Shows URL Instead of Custom Text
entry.content:
Dear Community,

 

I am doing an enhancement in My Inbox app, where I want to add a url to a specific item row.  Here I am using two calculated fields, one to determine the text and another to determine the URL to be used. Now I have done the following in my CDS:

@Semantics.url: {

mimeType: 'url'

}

@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_REVIEWINVOICE'

@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

cast (' ' as abap.char(1024) )

as WEBSITEURL,

@ui.lineItem: [ {

position: 14,

importance: #HIGH,

type: #WITH_URL,

label : 'Webside URL' ,

value: 'REVIEWINVOICE' , //what users sees

url: 'WEBSITEURL' //actual link

} ]

@Semantics.url: {

mimeType: 'url'

}

@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_REVIEWINVOICE'

@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

cast( ' ' as abap.char(1024) ) as REVIEWINVOICE,

 

I am trying to display the virtual calculated text message REVIEWINVOICE and show the other virtual calculated URL WEBSITEURL when clicking. Problem is the value and the text seem to overwrite each other somehow. The URL loading seems to work only when i put WEBSITEURL in the value section.

Additionally if I comment the @Semantics.url for REVIEWINVOICE, I cannot click on the link anymore.

Any Ideas ideas what am I doing wrong here. I wanted to mention that in IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ~CALCULATE, I manage determine both the text REVIEWINVOICE and the URL for WEBSITEURL

Thanks and looking forward for any ideas on this topic.

entry.title: RAP‑Based Service Does Not Support Aggregation Totals with Filters and Group‑By with Crud
entry.content:
Problem Description:

We are implementing a Fiori Elements List Report using a RAP‑based OData service (OData V2 or V4).
The business requirement is to show aggregated totals (SUM) for multiple numeric columns. These totals must update dynamically whenever:

  • Filters are applied
  • Group By is enabled
  • Group levels expand/collapse
  • Sorting or table personalization is applied

Most Important Requirement:

When the List Report table is grouped, the system must display an aggregation subtotal row for each group (group-level total), not only a grand total.

However, RAP currently does not allow analytical annotations or analytical CDS entities to be used with transactional behavior. Because of this limitation:

  • We cannot enable annotations such as
    • @analytics.query: true
    • @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
    • @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  • RAP services cannot perform backend aggregations or expose group-level totals.
  • As a result, neither grand totals nor group-level totals can appear in the List Report.

Observed Limitation:

Backend (RAP):

  • RAP business objects with behavior definitions do not support analytical CDS views.
  • Aggregations and group-by queries are not supported in RAP OData V2 or OData V4.
  • Analytical annotations cannot be used on projection or interface views participating in RAP.

entry.title: RAP‑Based Service Does Not Support Aggregation Totals with Filters and Group‑By
entry.content:
We are implementing a Fiori Elements List Report using a RAP‑based OData service (OData V2 or V4).
The business requirement is to show aggregated totals (SUM) for multiple numeric columns. These totals must update dynamically whenever:

  • Filters are applied
  • Group By is enabled
  • Group levels expand/collapse
  • Sorting or table personalization is applied

    Most Important Requirement:

    When the List Report table is grouped, the system must display an aggregation subtotal row for each group (group-level total), not only a grand total.

    However, RAP currently does not allow analytical annotations or analytical CDS entities to be used with transactional behavior. Because of this limitation:

    • We cannot enable annotations such as
      • @analytical.query: true
      • @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      • @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      • RAP services cannot perform backend aggregations or expose group-level totals.
      • As a result, neither grand totals nor group-level totals can appear in the List Report.
